 1.Establish the “Other receivables” account for grants that were not yet liquidated and ensure that succeeding transactions will be debited to “Other receivables;”
 2.Issue notices to clients who have yet to liquidate their claims; and
3.Maintain file copies of the livelihood project monitoring reports and attach the same along with their corresponding liquidation reports to support the Journal Entry Vouchers for liquidation.

observations.recommendation:

•Submission of JEVs and its corresponding supporting documents;
•Strictly follow the General Accounting Plan for Local Government Units; and
•Update Subsidiary Ledgers.

Submitted JEVs are still for inventory.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to:

1. Conduct thorough review of POW’s and other technical aspects of the projects.
2. Follow procedures provided by law on contractors which cannot complete/implement the projects, such as the rescinding of the contracts and blacklisting.
3. Send letters to contractors to expedite the completion of projects.
observations.reason: Upon checking the Notes to Financial Statements, we observed that there are long outstanding Advances to Contractors relating to projects that were allegedly absconded by contractors. Our audit team had already issued an audit query inquiring information related to the actions made by the management and is currently waiting for a reply.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management the following:
The Municipal Accountant should prepare a journal entry voucher to recognize the LGU receivable, with all the supporting documents including the working papers thereof. The working papers must present all the details and information needed in order for the proper preparation of the subsidiary ledger per client which will be maintained by the Municipal Accounting Office. The suggested accounting entry as presented below:

Accounts Receivable 
    1,863,858.00

Prior period adjustments
   1,863,858.00

The Municipal Economic Enterprise and Development Office should submit a monthly report showing their actual receivables from the said ventures duly supported with complete schedules and copies of unpaid billing statements at the end of each month to the Municipal Accountant for proper recognition and recording.

The Municipal Treasurer should develop effective strategies and systems to expedite the collection of the ₱1,863,858.00.
observations.reason:
No working papers were submitted to this office.

Reports from the previous MEEDO were not submitted monthly.

The MTO had not submitted a strategic plan to mobilize collection efforts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to religiously update the subsidiary ledgers of the “Advances to Officers and Employees” account and assure that it will tally with the ending balance of ₱2,462,294.35 as of December 31, 2021 as starting point. 
The Municipal Accountant must immediately demand liquidations from the concerned officials and employees thru demand letter concerning their outstanding cash advances based on the updated subsidiary ledgers, otherwise, inform them, for those that haven’t been deducted yet, that the LGU will commence with the deduction against their payroll accounts once no liquidation reports will be received by the Municipal Accounting Office on a given period.

 Also, the Municipal Accountant must reconcile negative balances or the overpayments made by some officers and employees and refund them if warranted or else recognize them under “Other Income” account.

Further, management must submit the liquidation report of the intelligence and confidential fund immediately to the COA Chairman’s Office per COA Circular No. 2003-003 dated July 30, 2003.

Furthermore, we recommend to the management to strictly control the grant of cash advances and insure that previous cash advances are fully liquidated first before granting a new one. Also, the officials and employees must submit a liquidation report immediately once the purpose has been served.
observations.reason:
Collections are ongoing. 

Further, the audit team had already approached/ contacted connected employees and officials with pending liquidations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to do the following courses of action:

Trace back the records of the Due to GSIS account in the books of accounts of the LGU from the time huge outstanding balance of the account becomes apparent, identify the cause of its enormous figure and apply proper remedies like adjusting entries for errors, etc. as necessary to correct it.

Settle all unremitted premiums and loan remittances of the employees to GSIS Malaybalay City Branch immediately if there are any. 

Constantly update the LGU’s records/ledgers based on the current membership data and loan records and reconcile them with the records of GSIS Malaybalay City Branch regularly to assure correct, reliable and accurate collections and remittances of GSIS premiums and loan payments.
observations.reason: There remains a variance for reconciliation and subsequent remittance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to management the following courses of action:

a. The Municipal Accountant must immediately prepare the liquidation reports of the total variance concerning the foregoing projects and submit them to COA for receipt (stamped) and signature by the auditor in the Reports of Checks Issued (RCI), if applicable, and Statement of Expenditures (SOE) before forwarding it to the Provincial Accountant.

Further, with fund transfers, reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts between the source agency and LGU Lantapan must be done regularly in order to assure uniformity of the balances and to insure correctness, reliability and accuracy of the financial statements. If necessary, the Municipal Accountant should often request a copy of subsidiary ledger from the source agency for reference.

b. Management must immediately submit the hard copies of the various Memorandum of Agreements (MOA) of the previously cited projects to the Office of the Auditor for review and verification and for reference in conducting more audit procedures.
observations.reason: Lacking submission of liquidation from management. No MOA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Economic Enterprise and Development Officer (MEEDO) to impose the 5% interest/penalty beginning January, 2022 onwards to all unpaid bills including arrears, for some, of the concessionaire. 

He should compute for the corresponding penalties for the current and those long-time arrears, for some, and include them as part of the outstanding liabilities of the concessionaires to be collected. 

Further, the MEEDO should always coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer during the collection periods. He should always furnish the Municipal Treasurer with updated monthly reports pertaining to the delinquencies of the concessionaires for her reference.

Also, we recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to devise effective strategies and techniques to expedite the collection of the ₱ 1,346,609.00 plus the 5% interest/penalty of ₱ 67,330.45, approximately.
observations.reason: Ongoing coordination with the MEEDO and the concessionaires. The MEEDO swore to issue collection letters to clients with outstanding payables.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the required supporting documents such as Certificates of Completion or 100% Statements of Work Accomplished to COA immediately for further review and validation by the auditor. 

The Municipal Accountant must also consider the immaterial amounts that should be charged to outright expense and prepare an adjusting entry straight-away to correct it.
observations.reason: Zero coordination between the Accounting and Engineering Department.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the MHO to begin with the conduct of a physical inventory count at least semi-annually and prepare an inventory report thereafter to be submitted to COA for review and validation and to Accounting Office for reconciliation and accounting.

The MHO must always practice proper inventory recording system and must implement effective and essential control measures to safeguard the drugs and medicines of the LGU. The MHO must also assign different persons with the receiving and distribution of the stocks for check and balance. 

We also recommended to the Municipal Health Officer to coordinate with the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant in accomplishing and maintaining inventory related forms such as Inventory Stock Cards, SSMIs and Report of the Physical Count of Inventories. 

Further, the Municipal Health Officer must submit a written justification of the difference/variance which costed ₱1,145,662.10 as shown in Table 2 above duly supported with concrete proofs to avoid suspension and disallowance in audit.
observations.reason: Inaction of management

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management the following courses of action:
	The Municipal Mayor must organize a Local Road Network Inventory Committee to spearhead and oversee the conduct of physical count of all the municipal roads and bridges owned and maintained by LGU Lantapan and immediately prepare and submit to COA a Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network (RPCLRN) or Annex C of COA Circular 2015-008. The Inventory Committee must coordinate with the MPDO for the list of the municipal roads and update it for any additions.

The General Services Officer must facilitate the preparation and submission of Report on Local Road Network (RLRN) or Annex A and the maintenance of the Local Road Network Property Card or Annex E of COA Circular 2015-008. Other responsibilities of the General Services Officer are enumerated in the said Circular. 

The Municipal Accountant must reclassify the completed municipal roads and bridges that remained in the Construction in Progress account and record the results of the inventory of local roads and bridges conducted by the Local Road Network Inventory Committee to Road Network account. He must faithfully observed Annex B in disclosing the details to the Notes to Financial Statements and he must maintain and update regularly Annex C or the prescribed Local Road Network Ledger Card as mentioned in the foregoing Circular.

The LGU must closely observe the other significant provisions of COA Circular 2015-008 and execute them accordingly in order to properly account and report all the road networks owned and maintained by the LGU.
observations.reason: The Report on Physical Count of Local Road Network is yet to be prepared including Local Road Network Property Cards; Some roads and bridges are subject for reclassification to Road Networks.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Inventory Committee must conduct the required annual physical inventory of all PPEs owned by the municipal government, to verify the accuracy and completeness of reported balances of its PPE and inventory accounts. To facilitate the inventory taking, the GSO may require each office to conduct physical count of PPEs under their custody and prepare an inventory report that is complete in all particulars, such as item description, model, serial numbers and property numbers, and acquisition costs and dates, for submission to the Inventory Committee who in turn will validate the submitted inventory reports and come up with the consolidated RPCPPE.

We also recommend to require the Municipal Accountant must establish and determine the said inventory items so that if the condition warrants appropriate adjustments of the affected accounts shall be made upon proper documentation and approval.
observations.reason: The LGU already created an inventory committee, but they have yet to functions as intended.
Reconciliation of the negative balance was not taken up.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to trace back transactions in the previous years in order to determine the causes of the discrepancies of the reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjusting journal entries to reconcile the balances. The Municipal Accounts should take extra care in recording and posting of transactions involving reciprocal accounts.

We also recommend to require the Municipal Accountant to perform constant review of transactions to determine immediately any errors and omissions.
observations.reason: On-going reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to require the management to only pay honoraria to the NGO/PO PMC members and ensure that funds are used in accordance with rules, laws and regulations. Likewise, require the persons responsible/liable to refund the amount of P30,020.00.
observations.reason: The liability is not yet fully paid.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant should evaluate the propriety and validity of Accounts Payables and immediately settle those valid accounts and revert to unappropriated surplus those payables without valid/legal claims.
observations.reason: Considerable amount of the balance is still for reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant shall initiate or cause the verification of the nature of these accounts, determine their validity, and initiate the filing of the proper claims to concerned agencies and NGOs/POs.
observations.reason: Lack of relevant documents create difficulties in implementing the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to establish and determine these inventory items so that if the condition warrants appropriate adjustments of the affected accounts shall be made upon proper documentation and approval.
observations.reason: Inaction of Management

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation/s:
We recommended that the accounting office monitor the completion of projects to ensure that timely reclassification of assets is done. In addition, we advise the Municipal Accountant to evaluate the transactions and consider necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of the accounts, if warranted.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended that the management re-evaluate the transactions related to the rehabilitation of roads and, if warranted, draw up a JEV to record the necessary correcting entry.
We further recommended to the management that thereafter, all future projects that qualify as regular maintenance shall be directly recorded as an expense.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended that the management:
Monitor the project, particularly the liquidation of the Barangay, with regard to the grant of financial assistance for the purchase of a lot (cemetery). Require the submission of the SORD and the same should be attached to the journal entry voucher crediting the account ‘Due from LGUs.’ in line with the stipulation in the Memorandum of Agreement.
Require the Accountant to adjust the balances of the Due from LGUs immediately upon receipt of the SORD of the Barangay.
Assign/designate an individual who would be tasked to monitor all financial initiatives and their respective liquidation.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended that the management submit a justification to clarify the issues noted. Also, we require them to issue the necessary guidelines in prospective dole-out initiatives. The guidelines should carefully outline the purpose, eligibility characteristics and requirements, procedures, and the necessary supporting documents of the program/s. This audit representation would also like to solicit the affidavit of oneness of the persons with differently spelled names in the payroll and the certified list of affected farmers. Immediate distribution or refund of the pending balance of P15,000.00 is also advised. Moreover, we would like to call the accountant's attention to re-evaluate the accounting entries and assess whether the recording of an adjusting entry is still warranted.
Regarding the general policy on cash advances, we would like to remind the disbursing officers to adhere to the general policy relating to the grant and liquidation of cash advances. We invited their attention to the provision that mandated that all cash advances should be liquidated by yearend.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended that the management re-evaluate the transactions related to the rehabilitation of roads and, if warranted, draw up a JEV to record the necessary correcting entry.
We further recommended to management that, thereafter, elements of the cost of PPE should be carefully considered and directly capitalized.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended that the management establish clear internal rules and procedures with regard to the proper computation of the BAC and TWG honoraria. We also advise that the rules should include the important variables to be considered like attendance, regular work and designation, and others. Lastly, we prospectively require the management to attach the detailed computation of the honoraria along with the internal rules taken into consideration. This recommendation is consistent with Section 4 of the DBM Budget Circular 2004-5A.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended the management to be more circumspect and diligent in issuing Certificates of Acceptance. Also, they should strictly observe the defects liability period to safeguard the interests of the government.
For those projects with prematurely issued Certificate of Acceptance, require the contractor to post a warranty security as stipulated in the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended the management to submit the lacking documents to complete the audit process.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended to the management to ensure that all SWAs emanate from the contractor or, if changes/variances/adjustments will be made by the management and opt to prepare a new SWA, always secure the contractor's signature in the conforme portion.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended management to:
Inquire about the status of completion of the erected structures and prospective plans of the fund source agencies.
Clarify details about the turnover of the Health Center at Barangay Cawayan from the DOH and the Barangay and procure the necessary documentation.
Coordinate with the respective fund source agencies/external parties about the Memoranda of Agreement/Understanding (MOA/U) of the erected structures and solicit copies of the perfected MOA/U.
If there are no MOA/Us available from the respective fund source agencies, the LGU and the respective fund source agencies/external parties should execute Memoranda of Agreement/Understanding about the erected structures to define ownership, rights, responsibilities, timelines, and etc. of the said structures.
Ensure succeeding use of LGU land properties by other agencies or external parties are duly supported by favorable MOA/Us.
Ascertain that all projects including those funded by other agencies or parties are integrated in the local development plans of the LGU.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended management to:
Create detailed guidelines (complete with process flow, eligibility criteria, documentary requirements, distribution guidelines, crowd management scheme, internal control mechanisms to prevent repeat claims, and liquidation and monitoring) for all inventory distribution programs like Hatud Serbisyo;
Submit explanation/justification and/or substantiating evidence to support the:
variances or under distribution of goods totaling P588,586.43;
differences in the number of beneficiaries in the summaries as compared with the distribution sheets
receipts of goods of the target beneficiaries
goods distributed to different households vary in some instances
distribution sheets that are not meticulously monitored during the distribution phase which resulted to incomplete data
repeat claims of claimants which affect targets and quantity of goods distributed to other households
Submit the complete distribution sheets and program designs/summaries of inventory purchases covered by Check Nos. 86851595, 86851685, and 86851866;
Submit authorization letters signed by beneficiaries and confirmed by representatives related to receipts of goods with same signatures;
Ensure that succeeding Purchase Orders should contain brands and detailed quantity of goods contained in each box, case, sack, or the like; and
Instruct the inspectorate team to check the quantity of goods contained in each case/box/sack via manual count or weighing.
We recommended the LGU to (1) communicate and solicit comments about the issue to the Source Agency, (2) Submit Memoranda of Agreement whenever available.

We recommended the following to management:
•	Prepare Reports of Disbursement for all succeeding cash advance transactions of the disbursing officer/s; and
•Maintain separate cashbooks for different cash advances.

We recommended to the Municipal Engineer to review all quarterly reports prior to submission to fortify reliability of reports. Also, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer should coordinate in monitoring the work accomplished related to this project and their corresponding progress payments.

We recommended to the BAC to submit the requested eligibility documents of these transactions and to ensure that succeeding procurement via Negotiated Procurement are sufficiently substantiated with all necessary documents required under the R-IRR of RA 9184.
We recommended to the Municipal Engineer to ensure that all As Built Plans of future projects (by admin or by contract) should reflect the actual structures built.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant (1) to monitor the recoupment deduction for the project, DTI-IFAD-RAPID Improvement of Kaatuan FMR to ensure that the variance is applied for recoupment deduction in the succeeding progress payments of the project and (2) to ensure that progress payments and recoupment deductions are correctly computed.

We recommended the Management to enhance the internal control on the grants of cash advances. Ensure that all cash advances are duly substantiated with relevant documentary requirements. 

We also recommended that management use automatic debit to account (ADA) for benefits and any payroll related expenditures to the payor.

We recommended the management to:

1.Submit the lacking documents of the afore-cited projects particularly those that are still ongoing; and
2.Prohibit extension of work of projects with lacking documents and compute the necessary liquidated damages if requirements of the provision are not strictly followed.
3.Review the procedural requirements before granting time extension and follow them.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to correct their erroneous recording of their assistance to the Philippine Red Cross Bukidnon Chapter by setting up “Due from Non-Government Organizations/People’s Organizations” for the donated amount in case there are still no liquidation received for the various accomplishments of PRBC. Further, we recommended that the LGU should compel the other party to liquidate in accordance to MOA.
observations.reason: Due to management efforts, Red Cross already submitted liquidation documents for half of the granted financial assistance (P300,000) to them. However, submission of these documents to COA is not yet done. Also, the setting up of the receivable account in our books is not done.

The Municipal Accountant must reclassify the completed municipal roads and bridges that remained in the Construction in Progress account and record the results of the inventory of local roads and bridges conducted by the Local Road Network Inventory Committee to Road Network account. He must faithfully observed Annex B in disclosing the details to the Notes to Financial Statements and he must maintain and update regularly Annex C or the prescribed Local Road Network Ledger Card as mentioned in the foregoing Circular.

The LGU must closely observe the other significant provisions of COA Circular 2015-008 and execute them accordingly in order to properly account and report all the road networks owned and maintained by the LGU.
observations.reason: The Report on Physical Count of Local Road Network is yet to be prepared including Local Road Network Property Cards; Some roads and bridges are subject for reclassification to Road Networks.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Inventory Committee must conduct the required annual physical inventory of all PPEs owned by the municipal government, to verify the accuracy and completeness of reported balances of its PPE and inventory accounts. To facilitate the inventory taking, the GSO may require each office to conduct physical count of PPEs under their custody and prepare an inventory report that is complete in all particulars, such as item description, model, serial numbers and property numbers, and acquisition costs and dates, for submission to the Inventory Committee who in turn will validate the submitted inventory reports and come up with the consolidated RPCPPE.

We also recommend to require the Municipal Accountant must establish and determine the said inventory items so that if the condition warrants appropriate adjustments of the affected accounts shall be made upon proper documentation and approval.
observations.reason: The LGU already created an inventory committee but they have yet to functions as intended.
Reconciliation of the negative balance was not taken up.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to trace back transactions in the previous years in order to determine the causes of the discrepancies of the reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjusting journal entries to reconcile the balances. The Municipal Accounts should take extra care in recording and posting of transactions involving reciprocal accounts.

We also recommend to require the Municipal Accountant to perform constant review of transactions to determine immediately any errors and omissions.
observations.reason: On-going reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to require the management to only pay honoraria to the NGO/PO PMC members and ensure that funds are used in accordance with rules, laws and regulations. Likewise, require the persons responsible/liable to refund the amount of P30,020.00.
observations.reason: The liability is not yet fully paid.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant should evaluate the propriety and validity of Accounts Payables and immediately settle those valid accounts and revert to unappropriated surplus those payables without valid/legal claims.
observations.reason: Considerable amount of the balance is still for reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant shall initiate or cause the verification of the nature of these accounts, determine their validity, and initiate the filing of the proper claims to concerned agencies and NGOs/POs.
observations.reason: Lack of relevant documents create difficulties in implementing the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to establish and determine these inventory items so that if the condition warrants appropriate adjustments of the affected accounts shall be made upon proper documentation and approval.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management the following actions:
Establish the “Other receivables” account for grants that were not yet liquidated and ensure that succeeding transactions will be debited to “Other receivables;”
Issue notices to clients who have yet to liquidate their claims; and
Maintain file copies of the livelihood project monitoring reports and attach the same along with their corresponding liquidation reports to support the Journal Entry Vouchers for liquidation.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to create a reconciliation statement to breakdown the variance observed and to continuously prepare reconciliation reports for the succeeding financial statement variances.

We hereby recommended the following to the management:
Submission of JEVs and its corresponding supporting documents;
Strictly follow the General Accounting Plan for Local Government Units; and
Update Subsidiary Ledgers.
We recommended the LGU to (1) communicate and solicit comments about the issue to the Source Agency, (2) Submit Memoranda of Agreement whenever available.

We recommended the following to management:
Prepare Reports of Disbursement for all succeeding cash advance transactions of the disbursing officer/s; and
Maintain separate cashbooks for different cash advances.

We recommended to the Municipal Engineer to review all quarterly reports prior to submission to fortify reliability of reports. Also, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer should coordinate in monitoring the work accomplished related to this project and their corresponding progress payments.

We hereby recommended the management to:
Submit the lacking documents immediately;
Set-up monitoring templates for received indorsements; and
Coordinate with the key offices to facilitate compliance of required documents.

We recommended to the management:
Submit the lacking documents
Facilitate transfer of the ownership in the Tax Declarations in favor of the Municipal Government;
Expedite the titling of the land properties; and
Strictly follow existing laws and guidelines for subsequent purchases of land and avoid paying the owner/s of the land before the documents are complete.

We recommended to the BAC to submit the requested eligibility documents of these transactions and to ensure that succeeding procurement via Negotiated Procurement are sufficiently substantiated with all necessary documents required under the R-IRR of RA 9184.
We recommended to the Municipal Engineer to ensure that all As Built Plans of future projects (by admin or by contract) should reflect the actual structures built.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant (1) to monitor the recoupment deduction for the project, DTI-IFAD-RAPID Improvement of Kaatuan FMR to ensure that the variance is applied for recoupment deduction in the succeeding progress payments of the project and (2) to ensure that progress payments and recoupment deductions are correctly computed.

We recommended the Management to enhance the internal control on the grants of cash advances. Ensure that all cash advances are duly substantiated with relevant documentary requirements.
We also recommended that management use automatic debit to account (ADA) for benefits and any payroll related expenditures to the payor.

We recommended the management to:
Submit the lacking documents of the afore-cited projects particularly those that are still ongoing; and
Prohibit extension of work of projects with lacking documents and compute the necessary liquidated damages if requirements of the provision are not strictly followed.
Review the procedural requirements before granting time extension and follow them.
We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to record retention money transactions under Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable account instead of Other Payables.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to ensure that financial reports are comparative and should reflect the actual transactions of the LGU. Also, the accounting office should only maintain one subsidiary ledger per Cash in Bank account.

We recommended to the BAC to require the submission of the PRC-authenticated valid IDs of the key personnel of the project to assist in the verification of the credentials of the declared manpower and to ensure that, moving forward, the bid evaluation and post-qualification procedures should be done with extra diligence to avoid data inconsistencies and omissions.

We recommended to the BAC to be more mindful of the PRPD procurement timeline for succeeding PRDP procurement procedures to ensure the timely implementation of the project initiatives. This recommendation holds true for other procurement initiatives of the LGU.

We recommended to the HRMO and the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of supporting documents on the monthly claims of salaries and other allowances of the Officials and Employees of the LGU. In addition, we are requiring the management to submit the lacking documents relating to all claims of Personnel Benefits for CY 2022.

We recommended to the management to establish a guideline complete with detailed definition of terms, documentary requirements, thresholds, and etc. on the grant of AIFCS to indigents using the DSWD Memorandum Circulars as reference. We further recommended that the principles of fairness and objectivity should be injected into the new system to be created regarding the grant of financial assistance.

We recommended to the Management to require warranty securities from the supplier of procured goods which should be covered by either a retention money or a special bank guarantee equivalent to the rates prescribed under RA9184. Furthermore, in order to avoid confusion, such requirement should already be disclosed to the bidders during the bidding process and be expressly included in the contract.

We recommended to the management to provide written explanation and justification of all the above-mentioned findings and observations. Also, we advised that the BAC and the TWG should be more careful in evaluating the documents of the bidders in all procurement activities.

We recommended to the management to submit the lacking documents in order to complete our audit of the transaction and ensure that succeeding grants of SRI benefits are complete with supporting documents and detailed computations for receipts of SRI that are different from the usual amount received by the majority.

We recommended to the management to submit the lacking documents (Copies of Appointments/Contracts of Services that are still effective as of December 15, 2022, Certified list of eligible JOs with their corresponding actual dates of start of work by the HRMO, and acknowledgment receipt relating to the unsigned claims).

We recommended to management the following courses of action:

a. The Municipal Accountant must coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in gathering the required documentary supports such as Certificates of Project Completion and Turn-over or 100% Statements of Work Accomplished (SWA), Certificate of Acceptance, Inspection Reports and the like of the ₱ 50,955,923.06 adjusting entries and submit them to COA immediately for further review and validation by the auditor. 

b. The Municipal Accountant must always support every adjusting entry with proper and complete documentary requirements in order to establish its correctness, validity and legality.

We recommended to management the following courses of action:

The Municipal Accountant must substantiate the paid disbursement vouchers with all the additional documentary requirements abovementioned, as applicable, on all future purchases related to repairs and maintenance of heavy equipment and motor vehicles.

The BAC Secretariat Head must coordinate with the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) and Municipal Engineer in complying and submitting the first additional documentary requirement above-cited to the Office of the Auditor on future procurements related to repair and maintenance of heavy equipment and motor vehicles within five days from the execution of contract or purchase order.

Create an Inspectorate team or an in-charge to conduct pre-repair evaluation/inspection and post repair evaluation/inspection of all its government vehicles and heavy equipment subjected to repair and maintenance.

 A ledger must be maintained by the Municipal Accounting Office for the expenses incurred regarding repair and maintenance of every motor vehicle or heavy equipment owned by LGU Lantapan.

We recommended to the Municipal Economic Enterprise and Development Officer (MEEDO) to draft, maintain and keep the 1,378 Water Service Contracts of Lantapan Urban Water Supply and Sanitation System (LUWASS). Each contract should be notarized by a lawyer and duly signed by the active concessionaire and the Municipal Mayor. This ensures the validity and legality of the water connections and serves as a protection of the LGU from aggravation whenever there will be possible conflicts between the engaging parties that will arise in the near future.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the required supporting documents such as Certificates of Completion or 100% Statements of Work Accomplished to COA immediately for further review and validation by the auditor. 

The Municipal Accountant must also consider the immaterial amounts that should be charged to outright expense and prepare an adjusting entry straight-away to correct it.
observations.managementAction: Management is still searching for the required documents.
observations.reason: Non-submission of the required supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the OIC - Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to submit the Utilization Monitoring Report to COA for review and validation by the auditor and to ascertain that the cash donations were truly spent for the livelihood projects abovementioned.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO is still checking its records for the said Utilization Monitoring Report.
observations.reason: Non-submission of the Utilization Monitoring Report.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant should evaluate the propriety and validity of Accounts Payables and immediately settle those valid accounts and revert to unappropriated surplus those payables without valid/legal claims.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will continue to look for feasible remedies to address the problem.
observations.reason: The Accountant was not able to revert the balances in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to management the following courses of action:
Management must see to it that financial assistance received from the PGB or any other agency shall only be used for the specific purpose it was intended for and must refrain from investing them to high-yield savings account;
Management must expedite and must avoid delay in the commencement and full implementation of target projects of financial assistance received from the PGB to avoid the risk of termination and cancellation of financial assistance as stipulated in the MOA; and
The Municipal Accountant must assure that Cash-in-Bank accounts are properly classified and reported in the Financial Statements.

We recommended to the Municipal Treasurer the following:
To ensure that the ₱ 2,441,125.45 was returned to the actual funding of the affected Trust Fund projects.
To strictly adhere to Section 305 (e) of R.A. 7160 that Trust funds shall only be used in fulfillment of the specific purpose for which the trust was created or received.
To refrain from borrowing from other Trust Funds to avoid disruption with their implementation and timely completion.

We recommended to the management the following:
The Municipal Accountant should prepare a journal entry voucher to recognize the LGU receivable, with all the supporting documents including the working papers thereof. The working papers must present all the details and information needed in order for the proper preparation of the subsidiary ledger per client which will be maintained by the Municipal Accounting Office. The suggested accounting entry as presented below:
Accounts Receivable				1,863,858.00
Prior period adjustments				1,863,858.00
The Municipal Economic Enterprise and Development Office should submit a monthly report showing their actual receivables from the said ventures duly supported with complete schedules and copies of unpaid billing statements at the end of each month to the Municipal Accountant for proper recognition and recording.
The Municipal Treasurer should develop effective strategies and systems to expedite the collection of the ₱ 1,863,858.00.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to religiously update the subsidiary ledgers of the “Advances to Officers and Employees” account and assure that it will tally with the ending balance of ₱ 2,462,294.35 as of December 31, 2021 as starting point. The Municipal Accountant must immediately demand liquidations from the concerned officials and employees thru demand letter concerning their outstanding cash advances based on the updated subsidiary ledgers, otherwise, inform them, for those that haven’t been deducted yet, that the LGU will commence with the deduction against their payroll accounts once no liquidation reports will be received by the Municipal Accounting Office on a given period. Also, the Municipal Accountant must reconcile negative balances or the overpayments made by some officers and employees and refund them if warranted or else recognize them under “Other Income” account.
Further, management must submit the liquidation report of the intelligence and confidential fund immediately to the COA Chairman’s Office per COA Circular No. 2003-003 dated July 30, 2003.
Furthermore, we recommended to the management to strictly control the grant of cash advances and insure that previous cash advances are fully liquidated first before granting a new one. Also, the officials and employees must submit a liquidation report immediately once the purpose has been served.

We recommended to the management the following courses of action:
The Municipal Accountant must coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in gathering the required documentary supports such as Certificates of Project Completion and Turn-over or 100% Statements of Work Accomplished (SWA), Certificate of Acceptance, Inspection Reports and the like of the ₱ 50,955,923.06 adjusting entries and submit them to COA immediately for further review and validation by the auditor.
The Municipal Accountant must always support every adjusting entry with proper and complete documentary requirements in order to establish its correctness, validity and legality.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to do the following courses of action:
Trace back the records of the Due to GSIS account in the books of accounts of the LGU from the time huge outstanding balance of the account becomes apparent, identify the cause of its enormous figure and apply proper remedies like adjusting entries for errors, etc. as necessary to correct it.
Settle all unremitted premiums and loan remittances of the employees to GSIS Malaybalay City Branch immediately if there are any.
Constantly update the LGU’s records/ledgers based on the current membership data and loan records and reconcile them with the records of GSIS Malaybalay City Branch regularly to assure correct, reliable and accurate collections and remittances of GSIS premiums and loan payments.

We recommended to the management the following courses of action:
The Municipal Accountant must immediately prepare the liquidation reports of the total variance concerning the foregoing projects and submit them to COA for receipt (stamped) and signature by the auditor in the Reports of Checks Issued (RCI), if applicable, and Statement of Expenditures (SOE) before forwarding it to the Provincial Accountant.
Further, with fund transfers, reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts between the source agency and LGU Lantapan must be done regularly in order to assure uniformity of the balances and to insure correctness, reliability and accuracy of the financial statements. If necessary, the Municipal Accountant should often request a copy of subsidiary ledger from the source agency for reference.
Management must immediately submit the hard copies of the various Memorandum of Agreements (MOA) of the previously cited projects to the Office of the Auditor for review and verification and for reference in conducting more audit procedures.

We recommended to the management the following courses of action:
The Municipal Accountant must substantiate the paid disbursement vouchers with all the additional documentary requirements abovementioned, as applicable, on all future purchases related to repairs and maintenance of heavy equipment and motor vehicles.
The BAC Secretariat Head must coordinate with the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) and Municipal Engineer in complying and submitting the first additional documentary requirement above-cited to the Office of the Auditor on future procurements related to repair and maintenance of heavy equipment and motor vehicles within five days from the execution of contract or purchase order.
Create an Inspectorate team or an in-charge to conduct pre-repair evaluation/inspection and post repair evaluation/inspection of all its government vehicles and heavy equipment subjected to repair and maintenance.
A ledger must be maintained by the Municipal Accounting Office for the expenses incurred regarding repair and maintenance of every motor vehicle or heavy equipment owned by LGU Lantapan.

We recommended to the Municipal Engineer the following:
Require the submission from the winning contractors the written consents from the bondsmen relative to the foregoing time extensions and submit them to COA for further review and validation.
Require the winning contractor to submit and attach a written consent from the bondsmen in addition to a letter request for time extension whenever there will be an extension of contract time in the future to ascertain that the new contract duration will be covered with the performance security in order to protect the interest of the government.

We recommended to the Municipal Agriculturist to submit a certification stating the amount of ₱ 209,118.00 as the total obligations of the farmers who availed the tractor services in CY 2021 noted by the Municipal Mayor supported with the Monitoring Ledger and all the copies of the “Letter of Intent to Rent Tractor Services”, if retrieval to all is still possible.
Further, the Municipal Agriculturist must start keeping and maintaining a copy of the “Letter of Intent to Rent Tractor Services” from now onwards to ascertain the correctness and accuracy of the succeeding income generated from the tractor rental by establishing the source document which is a reliable basis or reference. Additionally, the Municipal Agriculturist must improve its procedures and strengthen its control for the check and balance to assure that what is collected by the LGU is at par with the fees for the usage of the tractor.
Also, he should request for the immediate repair and restoration of the inoperative and defective tractor to cater more farmers and as an additional source of local revenues for the LGU.
Lastly, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Agriculturist must immediately reconcile the difference of ₱ 12,692.00 and must apply proper remedies if necessary.

We recommended to the management to draft an internal guidelines or an ordinance as to the grant of communication allowance to those who are entitled to it as mandated by law. It should contain and specify a fair and reasonable ceiling amount that is allowed to be claimed by the officials/heads concerned.
We also recommended to the District Supervisors to avail postpaid load plans not exceeding the ceiling amount to be established by the LGU with unlimited calls and texts and enough data for browsing for their cellular and internet lines instead of opting for prepaid ones in order to secure a Statement of Account (SOA) or Bill from the telephone company provider that will surely attest the true nature, which is for communication, of the expenditures. The SOA must always be part as documentary support in claiming the communication allowance. In such a way, audit suspension or audit disallowance, if warranted, is prevented.
Further, the District Supervisors must constantly and strictly adhere to the provisions in COA Circular No. 20212-001 and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in terms of complying with documentary requirements for common government transactions.

We recommended to the Municipal Economic Enterprise and Development Officer (MEEDO) to draft, maintain and keep the 1,378 Water Service Contracts of Lantapan Urban Water Supply and Sanitation System (LUWASS). Each contract should be notarized by a lawyer and duly signed by the active concessionaire and the Municipal Mayor. This ensures the validity and legality of the water connections and serves as a protection of the LGU from aggravation whenever there will be possible conflicts between the engaging parties that will arise in the near future.

We recommended to the Municipal Economic Enterprise and Development Officer (MEEDO) to impose the 5% interest/penalty beginning January, 2022 onwards to all unpaid bills including arrears, for some, of the concessionaires. He should compute for the corresponding penalties for the current and those long-time arrears, for some, and include them as part of the outstanding liabilities of the concessionaires to be collected.
Further, the MEEDO should always coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer during the collection periods. He should always furnish the Municipal Treasurer with updated monthly reports pertaining to the delinquencies of the concessionaires for her reference.
Also, we recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to devise effective strategies and techniques to expedite the collection of the ₱ 1,346,609.00 plus the 5% interest/penalty of ₱ 67,330.45, approximately.

We recommended to the management the following courses of action:
To submit a clear and definite legal basis and a written justification for the payment of the rice subsidy in CY 2021 which amounted to ₱ 1,966,650.00 to COA Office for further review and validation.
To refrain from paying its officials and employees with allowances, incentives and other fringe benefits not covered by existing and specific appropriations and have no clear and definite legal basis to prevent disallowance in audit as provided in COA Circular No. 2013-003 dated January 30, 2013.
To strictly observe the provision stipulated in DBM Budget Circular No. 2006-1 dated February 1, 2006 in the granting of rice allowance to LGU officials and personnel.

We recommended to the management the following courses of action:
To formulate and establish clear cut guidelines or policies thru an ordinance on the selection and grant of the LGU’s livelihood capital assistance to ascertain fairness and equality among the beneficiaries.
To submit a written justification to COA on the unequal distribution of the livelihood capital assistance in CY 2021 for further review and validation.
To discontinue the practice of preparing the project proposals for the beneficiaries and let them draft and submit them instead as a good control measure to avoid prejudice and inequality.

We recommended to the concerned collector to immediately prepare the RCD, encode the collections in the system, and remit the amount of ₱ 68,831.00 to the Liquidating Officer and deposit the same to the depository bank of the LGU. She must furnish the Office of the Auditor the certified true copies of the RCD and validated deposit slip. 

Further, we recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to closely monitor all collections made by the various collecting officers of the LGU, such as, to directly encode in the system all cash collections and remit the same to the Liquidating Officer on a daily basis in accordance to specific provisions cited in Presidential Decree 1445 and the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to adjust the ₱ 3.346 against Prior Period Adjustments and credit the “Advances to Contractors” account immediately to close them; Furnish COA of the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) duly supported with the proper documentary requirements.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry after completely substantiated with the proper documents in order to record a receivable, Due from Other Funds, and a liability, Trust Liabilities – DRRM, in the Trust Fund books amounting to ₱ 2,229,607.51. 

The Municipal Accountant must also insure that reciprocal accounts are reconciled at the end of the year. Further, the accounting personnel must always coordinate with each other as to their assigned accounting tasks in order to generate a very accurate and reliable financial statements.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry duly supported with necessary documents such as the physical inventory report and numerous Requisition Issue Slips (RIS), if available, or similar form from the MHO to account the unrecorded ₱ 357,343.50 worth of remaining food supplements/vitamins as of December 31, 2020.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry duly supported with the required documentary requirements to establish the transfer of ownership of motor vehicles from the LGU to the selected barangays and furnish COA of the copy of the JEV in taking up the donated assets to the barangays.

Further, the Municipal General Services Officer must relieve the Municipal Mayor from his accountability over the said assets by cancelling the various AREs that were issued to him.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to submit the required documentary supports of all the transactions listed in the schedule immediately to enable the auditor to continue with the validation of the year-end payables as to their existence, correctness and veracity, otherwise, make an adjusting entry and revert the same to surplus. 

We further recommended to the Municipal Accountant to insure that for the succeeding years Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) of accounts payable are duly supported with the required documentary requirements abovementioned. As an internal auditor of the agency, the required payable documents must be under his custody for safekeeping and must be readily available for review and verification upon request by the auditor to avoid suspension in audit.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to carefully review every accounting entry made before approving the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) in order to avoid errors from occurring and in order to provide a more apt and accurate financial reports.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to gather the lacking documentary requirements including the Applicable BIR Zonal Valuation and Certified Photocopy of Tax Clearance from Assessor’s Office where the land is located (Certificate Authorizing Registration, Capital Gains Tax, Documentary Stamp Tax and Transfer Tax) and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor to complete the post audit of the said transaction. 

We further recommended to the management to strictly complies to the requirements in Section 13.1 of the said COA Circular for future purchase of land in order to prevent suspension in audit.

We recommended to the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit the foregoing documentary requirements immediately for further validation by the auditor. The BAC must always exercise diligence in complying with the documentary requirements for the payment of honoraria as enumerated in COA Circular 2012-001 to avoid suspension and disallowance in audit.

We also recommended to the Municipal Accountant to consider only claims for honoraria with complete documentary requirements as discussed in the aforementioned circular.

We recommended to management the following courses of action:

Instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit a written explanation on (a) the negative variances in Item of Work: Road Reshaping (b) the 100% work accomplishment of the barangays without diesel obligations in Item of Work: Road Regraveling which includes Barangay Basac, Kaatuan, Kibangay, Kulasihan and Poblacion. Submit photographs and the like to prove that the said projects were fully completed.

For the succeeding projects, in case there are changes with the appropriations in the Detailed Estimates, the Municipal Engineer must revise the Program of Works (POW) immediately. Furnish the Municipal Budget Officer as her copy for the monitoring and control of the appropriations, the Municipal Accountant for proper accounting entries and the auditor as his reference in the conduct of post-audit. 

In the Purchase Request (PR) of every hard/infrastructure project of the LGU implemented by administration, the Municipal Engineer must indicate what Item of Work will it be used for as a reference for the monitoring of the appropriation by the Municipal Budget Office and, also, include the statement “Good as to Program of Works” in the PR to guarantee that there is no deviation from the original POW in the disbursement.

The Municipal Budget Officer must prepare an improved and detailed Project Monitoring Sheet showing appropriation for every Item of Work on projects implemented by administration to monitor its specific obligation as manifested in the Purchase Request from the Municipal Engineer and to avoid negative variance that might compromise the completion of other items/phases of a specific infrastructure project.

The Municipal Engineer must be specific with the description of the project in the Program of Works. Details like location of the project and, if necessary, measurements/dimensions such as linear meter for roads must be provided in the POW to be used as a reference in gauging the work accomplished.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer will continue to review every Program of Works of the LGU and its descriptions and will assure that they are specific.
observations.reason: Descriptions for some by-admin projects were not specific.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to gather the Distribution Sheets for NFA rice distribution and submit them to the Municipal Accountant for review and verification as to their completeness and correctness before forwarding them to COA Office for further validation by the auditor and to rationalize the immediate recording of “Welfare Goods Expense”.

We also recommended to the management to submit a written justification of the difference of 450 sacks/bags on the purchase of 1,600.00 sacks of NFA Rice and the non-submission of the Inspection and Acceptance Report (IAR). Submit the pertinent documents to validate your explanation, otherwise, we demand to refund for the amount equivalent to the variance of 450 sacks of NFA rice.

Also, the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) must insure that all deliveries must be inspected as evidenced by the Inspection and Acceptance Reports (IARs) to guarantee the safety of the assets of the LGU and to protect them from theft.

We recommended to management the following courses of action:

Instruct the MSWDO to submit all of the distribution sheets related to the foregoing to the Municipal Accountant for checking as to correctness and completeness of the documents and forward the same to COA Office for further review and validation by the auditor and to justify the recorded expense upon purchase.

The Requisitioning party must always prepare and submit an activity design before executing an activity or a procurement, if applicable. This will serve as a reference as to the needs and mechanics of the activity and a good basis for the auditor to determine if the purpose of the activity has been achieved or served.

Instruct the MSWDO to submit the validated list of the end-users or recipients of the foregoing; The MSWDO must always keep track of the monitoring report as to the quantity reached and composition of the food packs that will be dispersed in the incoming relief operations and furnished the same to COA office to avoid suspension and disallowance in audit.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the MHO to begin with the conduct of a physical inventory count at least semi-annually and prepare an inventory report thereafter to be submitted to COA for review and validation and to Accounting Office for reconciliation and accounting.

The MHO must always practice proper inventory recording system and must implement effective and essential control measures to safeguard the drugs and medicines of the LGU. The MHO must also assign different persons with the receiving and distribution of the stocks for check and balance. 

We also recommended to the Municipal Health Officer to coordinate with the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant in accomplishing and maintaining inventory related forms such as Inventory Stock Cards, SSMIs and Report of the Physical Count of Inventories. 

Further, the Municipal Health Officer must submit a written justification of the difference/variance which costed ₱ 1,145,662.10 as shown in Table 2 above duly supported with concrete proofs to avoid suspension and disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The MHO will start to observe the conduct of physical inventory of the existing and remaining drugs and medicines under its custody.
observations.reason: No semi-annual inventory report was submitted to COA Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management the following courses of action:

The Municipal Accountant must prepare adjusting entries to reclassify the ₱ 1.855 million to Construction Materials Inventory and revert it back to Construction-in-Progress or consider it as a prior period adjustment only when the project is 100 % complete as attested by the Municipal Engineer on the Certificates of Completion he will issue before closing it to its respective asset account or when they are fully utilized and totally exhausted, respectively.

The General Services Officer must oversee and monitor the acceptance, inspection, safekeeping and issuance of the said construction materials inventories. He must devise efficient and effective methods and develop related internal control measures such as maintenance of inventory ledger cards, preparation of Requisition Issue Slips (RIS), etc. to guarantee that everything will be fully accounted and taken care of. 

The Municipal Mayor must provide a secure and spacious warehouse that will accommodate and house the existing unutilized construction materials of the LGU.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to see to it that recording of similar transactions are made to proper accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009, and to make necessary adjusting entry to record the said cost of the project to its corresponding Construction-in-Progress account, and at the same time adjust the reported closed expense account against Prior Period Adjustment to reflect the correct balances.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to immediately record the total unrecorded land value of ₱ 16,836,990.00 in the books of accounts of LGU Lantapan in order to properly reflect the correct balance of the Land account in the Statement of Financial Position by preparing a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) duly and completely substantiated by the necessary supporting documents.

We recommended to the management the following courses of action:

To present the Certificates of Title (COTs) of the ₱ 464,000.00 worth of land (Appendix G of this audit report) as concrete confirmations for control and ownership. For those which have none, process and secure immediately their COTs in favor of the Municipality of Lantapan.

The Municipal Accountant must reconcile regularly with the Municipal Assessor’s register of lands to assure that everything that the LGU owns will all be accounted for and to guarantee that they are duly supported with the necessary documents in establishing their control and ownership.  
The safekeeping of the Certificates of Title must be assumed by the General Services Officer and must be filed in an orderly manner for easy review and verification when the need to do so arises in the near future. We recommended to the management the following courses of action:	
The Municipal Mayor must organize a Local Road Network Inventory Committee to spearhead and oversee the conduct of physical count of all the municipal roads and bridges owned and maintained by LGU Lantapan and immediately prepare and submit to COA a Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network (RPCLRN) or Annex C of COA Circular 2015-008. The Inventory Committee must coordinate with the MPDO for the list of the municipal roads and update it for any additions.

The General Services Officer must facilitate the preparation and submission of Report on Local Road Network (RLRN) or Annex A and the maintenance of the Local Road Network Property Card or Annex E of COA Circular 2015-008. The Municipal Accountant must reclassify the completed municipal roads and bridges that remained in the Construction in Progress account and record the results of the inventory of local roads and bridges conducted by the Local Road Network Inventory Committee to Road Network account. observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management the following courses of action:

The Municipal Accountant must submit liquidation reports of the foregoing financial transactions duly supported but not limited to the following documents: utilization and consumption schedules, distribution lists and acceptance reports.
 
Any procurement made by the LGU using the funds transferred by various National Government Agencies (NGAs) must be recognized and recorded initially as assets by the Municipal Accountant and be debited to Due to NGAs account only when they will be utilized, distributed or consumed or upon submission of liquidation reports duly substantiated with complete and proper documentary requirements.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to ensure that the 20 % Local Development Fund set for the year is fully utilized to finance priority programs, activities and projects of the LGU in line with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011. 

We further recommended to the Municipal Mayor to instruct the focal persons to expedite the implementation of all programs, activities and projects under the 20 % Local Development  including those in prior years in order for the constituents to relish the benefits and advantages that can be generated, upon completion, therefrom.

We recommended to the Local School Board (LSB) for the immediate realization of the programs and projects relative to the unutilized balance of ₱ 2,562,166.04 as embodied in the Local School Board Plan. Furthermore, the LSB must insure the timely implementation of the forthcoming appropriations for capital outlay as a way to show consideration of the students’ needs and to encourage them pursue their education and excel in their studies.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee must conduct the required annual physical inventory of all PPEs owned by the municipal government, to verify the accuracy and completeness of reported balances of its PPE and inventory accounts. To facilitate the inventory taking, the GSO may require each office to conduct physical count of PPEs under their custody and prepare an inventory report that is complete in all particulars, such as item description, model, serial numbers and property numbers, and acquisition costs and dates, for submission to the Inventory Committee who in turn will validate the submitted inventory reports and come up with the consolidated RPCPPE.

We also recommend to require the Municipal Accountant must establish and determine the said inventory items so that if the condition warrants appropriate adjustments of the affected accounts shall be made upon proper documentation and approval.

Instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit a written explanation on (a) the negative variances in Item of Work: Road Reshaping (b) the 100% work accomplishment of the barangays without diesel obligations in Item of Work: Road Regraveling which includes Barangay Basac, Kaatuan, Kibangay, Kulasihan and Poblacion. Submit photographs and the like to prove that the said projects were fully completed.
For the succeeding projects, in case there are changes with the appropriations in the Detailed Estimates, the Municipal Engineer must revise the Program of Works (POW) immediately. Furnish the Municipal Budget Officer as her copy for the monitoring and control of the appropriations, the Municipal Accountant for proper accounting entries and the auditor as his reference in the conduct of post-audit.
In the Purchase Request (PR) of every hard/infrastructure project of the LGU implemented by administration, the Municipal Engineer must indicate what Item of Work will it be used for as a reference for the monitoring of the appropriation by the Municipal Budget Office and, also, include the statement “Good as to Program of Works” in the PR to guarantee that there is no deviation from the original POW in the disbursement.
The Municipal Budget Officer must prepare an improved and detailed Project Monitoring Sheet showing appropriation for every Item of Work on projects implemented by administration to monitor its specific obligation as manifested in the Purchase Request from the Municipal Engineer and to avoid negative variance that might compromise the completion of other items/phases of a specific infrastructure project.
The Municipal Engineer must be specific with the description of the project in the Program of Works. Details like location of the project and, if necessary, measurements/dimensions such as linear meter for roads must be provided in the POW to be used as a reference in gauging the work accomplished.

We recommended to the management the following courses of action:
The Municipal Accountant must submit without undue delay to the Office of the Auditor the paid disbursement vouchers pursuant to the above-mentioned laws, rules and regulations.
The management cause the suspension of payment of the salaries of the employees responsible for the delayed submission or non-submission of the required reports until the same shall have been submitted.

We recommended to the management the following courses of action:
The Municipal Accountant must prepare adjusting entries to reclassify the ₱ 1.855 million to Construction Materials Inventory and revert it back to Construction-in-Progress or consider it as a prior period adjustment only when the project is 100 % complete as attested by the Municipal Engineer on the Certificates of Completion he will issue before closing it to its respective asset account or when they are fully utilized and totally exhausted, respectively.
The General Services Officer must oversee and monitor the acceptance, inspection, safekeeping and issuance of the said construction materials inventories. He must devise efficient and effective methods and develop related internal control measures such as maintenance of inventory ledger cards, preparation of Requisition Issue Slips (RIS), etc. to guarantee that everything will be fully accounted and taken care of.
The Municipal Mayor must provide a secure and spacious warehouse that will accommodate and house the existing unutilized construction materials of the LGU.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to see to it that recording of similar transactions are made to proper accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009, and to make necessary adjusting entry to record the said cost of the project to its corresponding Construction-in-Progress account, and at the same time adjust the reported closed expense account against Prior Period Adjustment to reflect the correct balances.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to immediately record the total unrecorded land value of ₱ 16,836,990.00 in the books of accounts of LGU Lantapan in order to properly reflect the correct balance of the Land account in the Statement of Financial Position by preparing a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) duly and completely substantiated by the necessary supporting documents.

We recommended to the management the following courses of action:
To present the Certificates of Title (COTs) of the ₱ 464,000.00 worth of land (Appendix G of this audit report) as concrete confirmations for control and ownership. For those which have none, process and secure immediately their COTs in favor of the Municipality of Lantapan.
The Municipal Accountant must reconcile regularly with the Municipal Assessor’s register of lands to assure that everything that the LGU owns will all be accounted for and to guarantee that they are duly supported with the necessary documents in establishing their control and ownership.
The safekeeping of the Certificates of Title must be assumed by the General Services Officer and must be filed in an orderly manner for easy review and verification when the need to do so arises in the near future. They must be kept in a staunch safe to save them from mutilation and unwarranted deterioration like the most common cases of termites’ infestation, fire infiltration and unforeseen soiling.

We recommended to the management the following courses of action:
The Municipal Mayor must organize a Local Road Network Inventory Committee to spearhead and oversee the conduct of physical count of all the municipal roads and bridges owned and maintained by LGU Lantapan and immediately prepare and submit to COA a Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network (RPCLRN) or Annex C of COA Circular 2015-008. The Inventory Committee must coordinate with the MPDO for the list of the municipal roads and update it for any additions.
The General Services Officer must facilitate the preparation and submission of Report on Local Road Network (RLRN) or Annex A and the maintenance of the Local Road Network Property Card or Annex E of COA Circular 2015-008. Other responsibilities of the General Services Officer are enumerated in the said Circular.
The Municipal Accountant must reclassify the completed municipal roads and bridges that remained in the Construction in Progress account and record the results of the inventory of local roads and bridges conducted by the Local Road Network Inventory Committee to Road Network account. He must faithfully observed Annex B in disclosing the details to the Notes to Financial Statements and he must maintain and update regularly Annex C or the prescribed Local Road Network Ledger Card as mentioned in the foregoing Circular.
The LGU must closely observe the other significant provisions of COA Circular 2015-008 and execute them accordingly in order to properly account and report all the road networks owned and maintained by the LGU.

We recommended to the management the following courses of action:
The Municipal Accountant must submit liquidation reports of the foregoing financial transactions duly supported but not limited to the following documents: utilization and consumption schedules, distribution lists and acceptance reports.
Any procurement made by the LGU using the funds transferred by various National Government Agencies (NGAs) must be recognized and recorded initially as assets by the Municipal Accountant and be debited to Due to NGAs account only when they will be utilized, distributed or consumed or upon submission of liquidation reports duly substantiated with complete and proper documentary requirements.

We recommended to the management to discontinue the practice of paying the BAC honoraria using the collected liquidated damages proceeds. Further, the agency must adhere strictly and consider only the aforementioned sources for the said remuneration for us not to tender a Notice of Disallowance.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to ensure that the 20 % Local Development Fund set for the year is fully utilized to finance priority programs, activities and projects of the LGU in line with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
We further recommended to the Municipal Mayor to instruct the focal persons to expedite the implementation of all programs, activities and projects under the 20 % Local Development  including those in prior years in order for the constituents to relish the benefits and advantages that can be generated, upon completion, therefrom.

We recommended to the Local School Board (LSB) for the immediate realization of the programs and projects relative to the unutilized balance of ₱ 2,562,166.04 as embodied in the Local School Board Plan. Furthermore, the LSB must insure the timely implementation of the forthcoming appropriations for capital outlay as a way to show consideration of the students’ needs and to encourage them pursue their education and excel in their studies.

We likewise recommend to require the MEO to retrieve the records of the infrastructure projects which were implemented from prior years and submit to the Municipal Accountant the Report of Completion and/or status report of the projects. For the ensuing project implementation, submit to the accounting office such Report of Completion upon completion of each project.
