observations.recommendation:
We recommended to:
Require the concerned Accountable Officers to liquidate immediately their cash advances and demand refunds of the unused balances, otherwise, cause the withholding of their salaries and wages to compel them to settle their account.
Exert extra efforts towards collections through cash settlements of long overdue unliquidated cash advances of officials and employees who are no longer connected with the municipality;
Request for write-off of long outstanding cash advances whose creditors are no longer located/deceased, if warranted; and
Enforce strict compliance to the provisions on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended the following:
That the Municipal Accountant/designated staff shall strengthen the internal controls over Cash in Bank account and make the necessary adjusting journal entry/ies to record the reconciling items for an accurate presentation of the financial statements;
That the Municipal Treasurer shall send a written notice to the payees or holder in due course of the stale check/s to surrender the check/s for further cancellation and the subsequent issuance of a new check/s; and
That the Municipal Accountant/designated staff shall make an adjusting entry for outstanding checks that has become stale and cannot be presented for payment in order to determine the correct balances of the Cash in Bank accounts and Accounts Payable.

We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to fast tract the updating of the required subsidiary ledgers and supporting schedules to support recorded transactions, and regularly conduct annual physical count of its properties and once every semester for the Inventories to determine existence and physical condition of the assets and to promptly make the necessary adjustments on some Inventory accounts.

We require that the Municipal Accountant to see to it that the Financial statements with supporting schedules and all other financial reports will be submitted on time or on before February 14 of the ensuing year, as required in Section 41 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2010-01.

We recommended that the Local School Board (LSD) shall:
Submit justification for the inclusion of the above items totaling P159,000.00 in the CY 2017 SEF appropriation.
Stop any procurement/disbursements in relation to the above identified items.
Comply with the provisions of DepEd, DBM, and DILG issued Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 and allocate expenses only for the items identified as chargeable to the SEF.
Avoid inclusion of items in generic term in the appropriation.

We recommended the following:
That the Local School Board (LSB) shall submit a written explanation for the non-implementation of various projects appropriated for CY 2017.
That the LSB shall implement immediately the programs and projects pertinent to the unutilized balances as embodied in the Local School Board Plan.

Require the LDRRM officer to strictly comply with the preparation and timely submission of the required monthly utilization report of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipality through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) shall:
Review and update the Municipal Solid Waste Management ten (10) Year Plan in accordance with the National Solid Waste Management Council (NSWMC) annotated outline as required under Section 16 of RA 9003; and
Speedy and strict implementation of the Municipal Ordinance No. 166 s. 2017 dated October 2, 2017.

Moreover, to ensure efficiency and effectiveness of solid waste management program the following are recommended:
Establishment of Multi-Purpose Environment Cooperatives or Associations.
Used of cellophanes by all business owners/operators should be discouraged.
Segregation of waste as to biodegradable and non-biodegradable should be strictly followed by the residents/commercial establishments.
Issuance of citation tickets to solid waste violators
Impose strict compliance on collection of biodegradable and non-biodegradable waste pursuant to Section 21 of R.A. 9003.
Strict imposition of “No Segregation, No Collection” Policy
Garbage should not be thrown in public places.

Further, we also require the Municipal Engineering Office to ensure proper and immediate construction of the Sanitary Landfill.

We recommended that management shall:
Require the Human Resource Management Officer to strictly require all the employees not on travel to strictly implement the use of the biometric system to monitor the time of attendance of all employees, including all personnel under job order status to achieve the intended purpose of the system. Also, the municipality should require all the personnel to use the system generated DTRs in lieu of the manually written DTRs.
Strictly implement the “No DTR, No Salary” policy.
